attacking_style: Opportunistic finisher who capitalizes on breakaway dynamics rather than solo long-range attacks. Strikes in final 150-200m when positioned correctly by teammates.
team_tactics: Relies on strong domestique delivery (Hamilton-led) to final meters. Exploits tactical disruptions and lack of cooperation among breakaway rivals to gain positional advantage.
terrain_preference: Maximum-distance flat to rolling stages where attrition favors tactical positioning over pure climbing or sprinting power. Breakaway scenarios over bunch finishes.
vulnerability: Requires teammate support for final positioning; lacks evidence of independent long-range attacking capability or ability to win from organized bunch sprints.
key_patterns: Wins from small front selections after ultra-long stages (212km+), showing endurance resilience.
Exploits tactical disorganization among breakaway rivals to gain numerical/positional advantage before finale.
Depends on domestique lead-out even in small groups; finished from Hamilton delivery within 150m.
form_trajectory: unknown: single observation from 2021 insufficient to assess trajectory or current form level
